zh.London, London, England: 美國密西根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦
zh.London St Lawrence Jewry with St Mary Ma: 英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 勞倫斯 也尤利 煙雨 聖 瑪麗 抹大拉的 米爾克 史崔特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London於大約1400年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，他的父親Sir Richard Rich31歲，母親Lady Catherine AUKER27歲。他於1424年與Catherine Cutherey在英國米德萊可倫敦結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭倫敦被列為"an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london.。他去世於1464年8月16日在他的家鄉享年65歲，埋葬在美國密西根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦。

zh-hans.London, London, England: 美国密歇根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London于大约1400年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，他的父亲Sir Richard Rich31岁，母亲Lady Catherine AUKER27岁。他于1424年与Catherine Cutherey在英国米德萊可倫敦结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他在英国英格兰倫敦被列为"an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london.。他去世于1464年8月16日在他的家乡享年65岁，埋葬在美国密歇根州門羅倫敦 堂史普倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Rich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a wealthy man (or perhaps in some cases an ironic nickname for a pauper), from Middle English, Old French riche ‘rich, wealthy’, a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to ancient Germanic rīc ‘power(ful)’.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rich, a pet form of any of the post-Conquest names beginning in Rich-, such as Richer and especially Richard . Compare Rick .

English: either a topographic name from Middle English riche(Old English ric) ‘stream’, signifying one who lived beside a stream, as at Glynde Reach (Sussex), or perhaps a habitational name from the (now lost) village of Riche (Lincolnshire).

variants.name: Ritchie
meanings: Scottish: from a Scottish and northern English pet form of the personal name   Rich  , a short form of   Richard  . This surname is also common in Ulster.

variants.name: Ritch
meanings: English and Scottish (Orkney): either a variant of Rich , or perhaps from a short form of Ritchie , itself a diminutive based on Rich.

Altered form of German Ritsch, probably from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on rīc ‘power(ful)’ or hrōd ‘fame, renown’; or an altered form of Swiss German Rütsch, Ruetsch, from Alemannic short forms of Rudolf .

variants.name: Riches
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): variant of Rich , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Richer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Armand, Fernand, Herve, Laurent, Marcel, Aurele, Donat, Elphege, Emile, Flore, Gilles.

English (East Anglia), French, and German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hari, heri ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rich(i)er, Ricier, but was largely absorbed by the much more common Richard .

Altered form of French Héricher: from the ancient Germanic personal name Harigari, composed of the elements hari ‘army’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’, or a variant of Hérissé, a nickname for someone with spiky hair, from the adjective hérissé, a derivative of the verb se hérisser ‘to stand on end’.

variants.name: Riche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Ghislaine, Philippe, Yanick, Yvon.
French: nickname for a rich man or perhaps an ironical name for a pauper, from Old French riche ‘rich’. Compare Leriche .

variants.name: Richins
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ricun, a diminutive of Rich , itself a pet form of Richard , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. It appears that a dialectally different (Norman or Picard) form of the French name, with medial /-k-/, also survived in England for a time alongside forms with original /-tʃ-/.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London was born about 1400, in London, Middlesex, England, his father, Sir Richard Rich, was 31 and his mother, Lady Catherine AUKER, was 27. He married Catherine Cutherey in 1424, in London, Middlesex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. His occupation is listed as "an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london. in London, England, United Kingdom. He died on 16 August 1464, in his hometown, at the age of 65, and was buried in London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London est né environ 1400, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Rich, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lady Catherine AUKER, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Catherine Cutherey en 1424, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est "an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london. à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 16 août 1464, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London è nato il circa 1400 nel London, Middlesex, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard Rich, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Lady Catherine AUKER, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Catherine Cutherey nel 1424, nel London, Middlesex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come "an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london. nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 16 agosto 1464, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Rich II, Sheriff of Londonが約1400年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Richは31歳，母Lady Catherine AUKERは27歳であった。1424年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンでCatherine Cuthereyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリスロンドンでの職業は"an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london.と記録されている。彼は1464年8月16日に故郷で65歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ミシガン州モンローロンドン トウンシュイップロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London(이)가 대략 1400년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Rich(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Catherine AUKER(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1424년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 Catherine Cutherey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 "an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london.이었다. 그는 1464년 8월 16일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 미시간 주 먼로 런던 타운시프 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London nasceu aproximadamente 1400, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Rich, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lady Catherine AUKER, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine Cutherey em 1424, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como "an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london. em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 16 de agosto de 1464, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London родился около 1400 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Rich было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Catherine AUKER, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Cutherey в 1424 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей.  Он работал по профессии "an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 16 августа 1464 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности лондон, лондон тоуншип, монро, Мичиган, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Rich II, Sheriff of London nació aproximadamente 1400, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Rich, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lady Catherine AUKER, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Catherine Cutherey en 1424, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era "an opulent mercer of london" mercer[edit] the term mercer for cloth merchants (from french mercier, "notions dealer") is now largely obsolete. mercers were formerly merchants or traders who dealt in cloth, typically fine cloth that was not produced locally. inventories of mercers in small towns, however, suggest that many were shopkeepers who dealt in various dry commodities other than cloth.[2] related occupations include haberdasher, draper and cloth merchant, while clothier historically referred to someone who manufactured cloth, often under the domestic system.  by the 21st century the word mercer was primarily used in connection with the worshipful company of mercers, one of the twelve great livery companies of the city of london. en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 16 de agosto de 1464, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en London, London Township, Monroe, Michigan, Estados Unidos.
